name: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC
subtitle: Steel Fabrication | Structural Connections — EN 1993-1-8:2005 §3.6, §3.7, §3.9, ISO 898-1:2013

inScope: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC
scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.label: Factored Shear Force on Bolt Group (V_Ed)

standards.name: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC
standards.description: High-Strength Bolt Group — Shear Capacity, Bearing Resistance & Slip Check per EC3 / AISC — Steel Fabrication, Structural Connections

formulas.expression: const As_map = {12:84.3, 16:157, 20:245, 24:353, 27:459, 30:561}; As_mm2 = As_map[bolt_diameter_mm]; const fub_map = {'8.8':800, '10.9':1040, '12.9':1220}; fub_mpa = fub_map[property_class]
formulas.reference: ISO 898-1:2013 — tensile stress areas; ISO 4014:2011 structural bolt dimensions

formulas.expression: gamma_M2 = 1.25; alpha_v = 0.6; Fv_Rd_kn = alpha_v * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * n_shear_planes / (gamma_M2 * 1000)
formulas.reference: EC3 §3.6.1 Table 3.4 — F_v,Rd = α_v·f_ub·A_s·n_sp/γ_M2; α_v=0.6 for 8.8/10.9 (shear plane through threaded portion)

formulas.expression: d0_mm = bolt_diameter_mm + 2; alpha_b = Math.min(e1_d0 = edge_dist_e1_mm / d0_mm / 3, p1_d0 = pitch_p1_mm / d0_mm / 3 - 0.25, fub_mpa / (plate_fy_mpa * 1.3), 1.0); k1 = Math.min(2.8 * 30 / d0_mm - 1.7, 2.5); Fb_Rd_kn = k1 * alpha_b * plate_fy_mpa * 1.3 * bolt_diameter_mm * connected_plate_t_mm / (gamma_M2 * 1000)
formulas.reference: EC3 §3.6.1 Table 3.4 — F_b,Rd = k_1·α_b·f_u·d·t/γ_M2; f_u ≈ 1.3·f_y (conservative for S235–S460)

formulas.expression: Fgov_single_kn = Math.min(Fv_Rd_kn, Fb_Rd_kn); phi_Rn_group_kn = n_bolts * Fgov_single_kn
formulas.reference: Group capacity = n × min(F_v,Rd, F_b,Rd) — EC3 §3.7 (no group reduction for n ≤ 6 in standard pitch)
DCR = V_Ed / R_group ≤ 1.0 required — EC3 §3.7 / AISC 360-22 §J3

formulas.expression: n_req = Math.ceil(V_Ed_kn / Fgov_single_kn); slip_check_kn = 0.7 * fub_mpa * As_mm2 * 0.5 / (gamma_M2 * 1000) * n_bolts
formulas.reference: n_req = V_Ed / F_single; slip resistance (cat. B) = 0.7·f_ub·A_s·μ/γ_M3 — EC3 §3.9 (μ=0.5 class A surface)

validation.message: Bolt group shear/bearing capacity exceeded — increase bolt count, diameter, or property class
End distance e_1 < 1.2 × d_0: EC3 §3.5 minimum violated — bolt will tear out of plate
Bolt pitch p_1 < 2.2 × d_0: below EC3 minimum spacing — risk of plate splitting between holes

warnings.message: Bearing governs at <60% of shear capacity: plate is thin or end/edge distance is short — increase plate thickness or end distance e_1 to balance resistances
Large bolt group in single shear (n > 8): EC3 §3.8 long joint reduction factor β_Lj applies when Lj > 15d — verify connection length does not trigger reduction
12.9 class bolt with thin plate: bearing governs severely — 12.9 bolts rarely needed in standard structural connections; verify whether 10.9 suffices
Utilisation > 85%: marginal for fatigue or seismic load — add one bolt or increase diameter; fatigue class detail per EC3-1-9 is independent of static capacity
Slip resistance insufficient for Category B (serviceability): connection will slip under service load — specify pretensioned bolts and improve faying surface to Class A (sand-blasted) μ=0.5

verdictText.warn: PASS — High Utilization: Peer Review Required

legalNotice: ⚠ Engineering decision support only — verify before use. This is a technical simulation, not professional engineering advice.
